section: Setting up an Argo CD instance
context: Managing GitOps deployment infrastructure
motivation: establish continuous deployment capabilities using GitOps
evidence: By default, {gitops-title} installs an instance of Argo CD in the `openshift-gitops` namespace... To manage cluster configurations or deploy applications, you can install and deploy a new user-defined Argo CD instance.
notes: Covers both default and user-defined Argo CD instances

job_statement: Install a user-defined Argo CD instance
section: Installing a user-defined Argo CD instance
context: Need to deploy applications or manage cluster configurations
motivation: manage cluster configurations or deploy applications in isolated namespaces
approach: Use the OpenShift web console to create an ArgoCD custom resource
acceptance_criteria: Argo CD instance is running; Route is created; Admin credentials are accessible
evidence: To manage cluster configurations or deploy applications, you can install and deploy a new user-defined Argo CD instance. By default, any new user-defined instance has permissions to manage resources only in the namespace where it is deployed.
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Prerequisites include cluster-admin access and GitOps Operator installed

job_statement: Create ArgoCD custom resource via web console
section: Installing a user-defined Argo CD instance
context: Installing Argo CD instance through UI
motivation: provision Argo CD instance with proper configuration
steps: Log in to web console; Navigate to Operators > Installed Operators; Select project; Click Create ArgoCD; Configure parameters; Enable Route; Create instance
evidence: Click *Create ArgoCD* to configure the parameters... Create an external operating system Route to access Argo CD server.

section: Installing a user-defined Argo CD instance
context: After creating Argo CD instance
motivation: verify the installation and access the Argo CD interface
steps: Go to Networking > Routes; Find instance route; Click web UI link; Authenticate using OpenShift or admin credentials
tools: OpenShift web console; Argo CD UI
evidence: Go to *Networking* -> *Routes*... On the *Details* tab, click the Argo CD web UI link... Use `admin` as the *Username* and the copied password

section: Setting up an Argo CD instance
context: Need to prevent automatic default instance creation
motivation: control which Argo CD instances run in the cluster
approach: Set DISABLE_DEFAULT_ARGOCD_INSTANCE environment variable in operator subscription
acceptance_criteria: Default Argo CD instance does not start in openshift-gitops namespace
evidence: To prevent the default Argo CD instance from starting in the `openshift-gitops` namespace, you can use the `openshift-gitops-operator` subscription and configure the `DISABLE_DEFAULT_ARGOCD_INSTANCE` environment variable
notes: Important for custom instance configurations within openshift-gitops namespace

job_statement: Configure Argo CD permissions and security
context: Multi-tenant cluster environments
motivation: reduce security risks and prevent privilege escalation
evidence: When you give namespaces to non-administrator users, for example, development teams, they can use the `namespace-admin` privileges... These roles are highly privileged and can delete all resources. To reduce this risk, configure common cluster roles with limited permissions
notes: Addresses security concerns with default admin role bindings

job_statement: Configure custom cluster roles for namespace-scoped instances
section: Configuring common cluster roles by specifying user-defined cluster roles for namespace-scoped instances
context: Delegating Argo CD instances to development teams
motivation: limit permissions and prevent privilege escalation
approach: Specify CONTROLLER_CLUSTER_ROLE and SERVER_CLUSTER_ROLE environment variables in operator subscription
alternatives: Inject variables directly into Operator Deployment object
acceptance_criteria: User-defined cluster roles are applied instead of default admin role
evidence: To configure common cluster roles for all managed namespaces, you can specify user-defined cluster roles for the `CONTROLLER_CLUSTER_ROLE` and `SERVER_CLUSTER_ROLE` environment variables
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Reduces risk of development teams gaining admin privileges

job_statement: Scale Argo CD components for high availability
context: Production deployments requiring high availability
motivation: distribute workloads and ensure service availability
evidence: To better distribute your workloads among pods, you can increase the number of Argo CD-server and Argo CD-repo-server replicas
notes: Argo CD-server and repo-server are stateless workloads

job_statement: Enable replicas for Argo CD server and repo server
section: Enabling replicas for Argo CD server and repo server
context: Need better workload distribution
motivation: improve availability and distribute load
approach: Set replicas parameters in ArgoCD custom resource spec
acceptance_criteria: Multiple replicas are running for server and repo-server components
evidence: Set the `replicas` parameters for the `repo` and `server` spec to the number of replicas you want to run
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Autoscaler overrides manual replica count if enabled

section: Deploying resources to a different namespace
context: Managing resources across multiple namespaces
motivation: allow Argo CD to manage resources outside its installation namespace
evidence: To allow Argo CD to manage resources in other namespaces apart from where it is installed, configure the target namespace with a `argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by` label
notes: Enables multi-namespace resource management

job_statement: Label namespaces for Argo CD management
section: Deploying resources to a different namespace
context: Granting Argo CD access to additional namespaces
motivation: extend Argo CD management scope beyond its installation namespace
approach: Apply argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by label to target namespace
acceptance_criteria: Argo CD can deploy and manage resources in labeled namespace
evidence: Configure the target namespace by running the following command: $ oc label namespace <target_namespace> argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by=<argocd_namespace>
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Simple label-based access control

job_statement: Customize Argo CD console link in multitenant clusters
section: Customizing the Argo CD console link
context: Multiple Argo CD instances in cluster
motivation: prevent confusion when multiple Argo CD instances exist
acceptance_criteria: Console link behavior matches administrator intent (enabled or disabled)
evidence: In a multitenant cluster, users might have to deal with many instances of Argo CD. After installing an Argo CD instance in your namespace, the Argo CD console link in the Console Application Launcher might open another Argo CD instance
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Controls whether console link is permanently or temporarily deleted

context: Managing container image updates and caching
motivation: control how and when container images are pulled
evidence: The {gitops-shortname} Operator lets administrators configure `imagePullPolicy` at multiple levels to control how Argo CD components pull container images
notes: Module type: CONCEPT; Hierarchical precedence: instance > global > default

context: Need consistent image pull behavior across all Argo CD instances
motivation: enforce consistent image update behavior
approach: Set IMAGE_PULL_POLICY environment variable in operator subscription
alternatives: Set at instance level using spec.imagePullPolicy
acceptance_criteria: All Argo CD instances use the configured pull policy
evidence: You can define a global image pull policy for all Argo CD instances managed by the Operator by setting the `IMAGE_PULL_POLICY` environment variable
notes: Module type: CONCEPT; Can be overridden at instance level

job_statement: Understand Argo CD custom resource structure
context: Configuring Argo CD instances
motivation: understand available configuration options
evidence: The `Argo CD` custom resource is a Kubernetes Custom Resource (CRD) that describes the desired state for a given Argo CD cluster and allows you to configure the components which make up an Argo CD cluster
notes: Module type: REFERENCE; Comprehensive property reference table

context: Managing multiple applications with similar patterns
motivation: automate multi-application management
approach: Configure applicationSet property in ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: ApplicationSet controller is enabled and configured
evidence: ApplicationSet Controller configuration options... enabled - The flag to use to enable the ApplicationSet Controller during the Argo CD installation
notes: Supports annotations, resources, log levels, source namespaces, webhook server

job_statement: Enable controller sharding for large-scale deployments
context: Managing large number of clusters
motivation: manage large numbers of clusters efficiently
approach: Configure sharding properties in controller spec
alternatives: Enable dynamic scaling based on cluster count
acceptance_criteria: Controller replicas are sharded; Memory pressure is reduced
evidence: sharding.enabled - Enable sharding on the Argo CD Application Controller component. Use this property to manage a large number of clusters and relieve memory pressure on the controller component
notes: Supports static replicas or dynamic scaling based on clusters per shard

context: Production deployments requiring redundancy
motivation: ensure service availability and fault tolerance
approach: Enable HA mode in ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: Redis HA proxy is deployed; HA is enabled globally
evidence: ha - High-availability options... enabled - Toggle high-availability support globally for Argo CD
notes: Includes Redis HAProxy configuration options

context: Controlling user access and permissions
motivation: control access and enforce authorization policies
approach: Define RBAC properties in ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: RBAC policies are enforced; Users have appropriate permissions
evidence: rbac - RBAC configuration options... defaultPolicy - The name of the default role that Argo CD falls back to when authorizing API requests... policy - CSV data about user-defined RBAC policies and role definitions
notes: Supports policy matcher modes (glob or regex) and OIDC scope configuration

context: Securing connections to Git repositories and external services
motivation: secure communications and establish trust
approach: Configure TLS and initialSSHKnownHosts in ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: TLS certificates are configured; SSH known hosts are defined
evidence: tls - TLS configuration options... ca.configMapName - The name of the `ConfigMap` which contains the CA certificate... initialSSHKnownHosts - Defines the initial SSH Known Hosts data for Argo CD
notes: Supports both HTTPS (TLS certs) and SSH (known hosts) connections

context: Customizing repo server behavior and capabilities
motivation: optimize manifest generation and repository interactions
evidence: The following properties are available for configuring the repo server component
notes: Module type: REFERENCE; Repo server handles manifest generation from Git repositories

job_statement: Configure TLS trust for repo server
section: Configure TLS trust for the repo server
context: Accessing repositories with custom CA certificates
motivation: enable repo server to trust custom certificate authorities
approach: Inject custom TLS certificates via secrets and config maps using systemCATrust
acceptance_criteria: Repo server trusts custom CAs; Plugin workflows can access TLS-enabled resources
evidence: You can configure the repo server to trust additional certificate authorities (CAs) by injecting custom TLS certificates into the repo server container and its Config Management Plugin sidecar containers
notes: Module type: CONCEPT; Supports wildcard certificates; Enables advanced plugin workflows

job_statement: Configure execution timeout for rendering tools
context: Using Helm or Kustomize with large manifests
motivation: prevent timeouts when rendering complex manifests
approach: Set execTimeout property in repo spec
acceptance_criteria: Rendering tools have sufficient time to complete
evidence: execTimeout - Execution timeout in seconds for rendering tools, for example, Helm or Kustomize
notes: Default is 180 seconds

job_statement: Set up notifications for Argo CD events
context: Tracking application sync status and failures
motivation: receive alerts when important events occur
evidence: Argo CD notifications allow you to send notifications to external services when events occur in your Argo CD instance. For example, you can send notifications to Slack or email when a sync operation fails
notes: Notifications are disabled by default

job_statement: Enable notifications for Argo CD instance
section: Enabling notifications with an Argo CD instance
context: Need to receive alerts about Argo CD events
motivation: receive alerts when events occur in Argo CD
approach: Set spec.notifications.enabled to true in ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: Notifications controller is enabled and running
evidence: To enable notifications for an Argo CD instance using the {OCP} web console... set the `spec.notifications.enabled` parameter to `true`
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Can enable via web console or CLI

context: Customizing notification messages and conditions
motivation: customize when and how notifications are sent
approach: Configure NotificationsConfiguration CR with templates, triggers, services, and subscriptions
acceptance_criteria: Notification templates are defined; Triggers are configured; Services are integrated
evidence: The `NotificationsConfiguration` resource is a Kubernetes custom resource (CR) that manages notifications... you can add templates, triggers, services, and subscription resources
notes: Module type: CONCEPT; default-notifications-configuration CR is created automatically

context: Defining notification message format
motivation: customize notification message content
steps: Edit NotificationsConfiguration CR; Add template under spec.templates; Define message format using Go templating
evidence: Templates are used to generate the notification template message... spec.templates: template.my-custom-template
notes: Uses Go templating with context variables like .app.metadata.name

context: Defining when notifications are sent
motivation: control when notifications are triggered
steps: Edit NotificationsConfiguration CR; Add trigger under spec.triggers; Define condition and templates to send
evidence: Triggers are used to define the condition when a notification is sent to the user and the list of templates required to generate the message
notes: Conditions use CEL-like expressions (e.g., app.status.sync.status == 'Unknown')

context: Integrating with external notification services
motivation: deliver notifications to external platforms
steps: Edit NotificationsConfiguration CR; Add service under spec.services; Configure service credentials and settings
evidence: Services are used to deliver a message... service.slack: token, username, icon
notes: Supports Slack, email, and other notification services

context: Multi-tenant environment where teams manage their own notifications
motivation: enable teams to manage their own notification settings
approach: Configure sourceNamespaces and notifications.sourceNamespaces in ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: Teams can manage notification configuration in their namespaces
evidence: By default, Argo CD manages notification configuration only within the control plane namespace. With {gitops-title} Operator, cluster administrators can enable teams to manage notification settings for their applications from additional namespaces
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Operator creates NotificationsConfiguration CR in each delegated namespace

section: Enabling Config Management Plugins in an Argo CD CR
context: Need to use tools beyond Helm, Jsonnet, and Kustomize
motivation: use custom config management tools or enable additional features
approach: Configure sidecar container in ArgoCD CR repo spec
acceptance_criteria: Config Management Plugin is running as sidecar; Custom tool is available
evidence: Argo CD provides support for Helm, Jsonnet, and Kustomize as built-in config management tools. To use a different config management tool, or to enable features not provided by the built-in config management tools, you can use the Config Management Plugin (CMP)
notes: Module type: CONCEPT; Plugin runs as sidecar container in repo server

context: Multiple Argo CD instances managing resources
motivation: prevent conflicts and enable parallel operation of multiple instances
evidence: {gitops-title} Operator enhances multi-instance support by improving annotation-based resource tracking in Argo CD.Multiple Argo CD instances can use annotation-based tracking by assigning each instance a unique `installationID`
notes: Module type: CONCEPT; Prevents resource tracking conflicts

job_statement: Configure annotation-based tracking in multiple instances
section: Configuring annotation-based tracking in multiple Argo CD instances
context: Multiple Argo CD instances with potentially identical application names
motivation: prevent conflicts and enable safe parallel operation
approach: Set unique installationID and resourceTrackingMethod in each ArgoCD CR
acceptance_criteria: Each instance has unique installationID; Resource tracking uses annotation+label; Instances operate without conflicts
evidence: Multiple Argo CD instances can use annotation-based tracking by assigning each instance a unique `installationID`, which enables them to correctly differentiate resources with identical application names, prevent conflicts, avoid infinite sync loops
notes: Module type: PROCEDURE; Must label namespaces to associate with correct instance
